title: bored with getting "You have launched an older version..."
creator: JonathanWexler
content:
First, let me describe the configuration I have been using successfully for two decades -

I am on the latest release channel release with a POP3 configuration.

I keep the message store on my file server and have Tbird installed on it and my laptop. The file server is a Win 11 machine as is my laptop. Each machine points to the message store (including the config file) on the file server.

Of late, every time I try to use Tbird on the file server, I get "You have launched an older version" message. So far reinstalling and pointing to the existing message store works.

But I am getting bored with having to reinstall every time, and I suspect this will eventually break completely.

Is there a way to stabilize this?

Thanks Jonathan
metadata: ;useragent:Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:150.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/150.0;tb_version:150.0 64bit;os:Windows 11

title: High CPU Usage on Linux
creator: FirefoxSync96
content:
I am running Thunderbird 140.9.1esr on openSUSE Tumbleweed.

For a long time now, Thunderbird often runs at high CPU (80-100% of one core), for several minutes at a time (5-10 minutes). This seems to happen many times every day, with no apparent cause from my end. (I just got back from Lunch, and the fan on my laptop was going crazy because of the high CPU.)

What can we do to identify and fix this issue?

title: Digital signing and encrypting : Where can I see/read what EXACTLY causes an "Invalid Signature" of/in an received mail ?
creator: willma.waswissen
content:
Dear all,

When receiving signed AND encrypted mails from an Outlook-account I get the exclamation sign for the signature.

The message is (German) : "Digitale Signatur ist ungültig Diese Nachricht enthält eine digitale Signatur, die aber ungültig ist. Die Nachricht wurde mit einer Verschlüsselungsstärke signiert, die von dieser Version Ihrer Software nicht unterstützt wird. Signiert von...."

I already did all standard checks (trusted, new hash algorithm and so on),

Now I would like to know the EXACT reason why Thunderbird is not accepting the digital signature. How can I accomplish this?

THX in advance and best regards!
